challenges.title: Hello Variables
challenges.description: Pick the correct Python variable assignment.

questionData.questionText: Which line correctly assigns 5 to x in Python?
questionData.explanation: Python uses = for assignment.

challenges.funFact: Python was named after Monty Python, not the snake.

challenges.title: Print Practice

questionData.questionText: Complete the code to print hello.
questionData.explanation: print() outputs text in Python.

challenges.funFact: The first computer bug was an actual moth found in a machine.

challenges.title: Bug Hunter 1

questionData.questionText: Fix the bug in this if statement.
questionData.explanation: Use == to compare values.

challenges.funFact: NASA still uses legacy code that predates modern web browsers.

challenges.title: List Length

questionData.questionText: How do you get the length of a list named nums?
questionData.explanation: len() returns the number of elements.

challenges.funFact: The term byte was coined in 1956 by Werner Buchholz.

challenges.title: Loop Starter

questionData.questionText: Fill the blank to start a loop.
questionData.explanation: Python uses for to iterate.

challenges.funFact: Early programmers often wrote directly in machine code.

challenges.title: String Quote Bug

questionData.questionText: Which fix closes the string properly?
questionData.explanation: Strings must have matching opening and closing quotes.

challenges.funFact: Ada Lovelace is often credited as the first programmer.

challenges.title: Boolean Check

questionData.questionText: Which is the correct Python boolean literal?
questionData.explanation: Python booleans are capitalized: True and False.

challenges.funFact: CAPTCHA was first used commercially in the early 2000s.

challenges.title: Function Name

questionData.questionText: Fill the keyword to define a function.
questionData.explanation: Functions are declared with def in Python.

challenges.funFact: Open-source projects power most of the modern internet.

challenges.title: Bracket Bug

questionData.questionText: Choose the corrected list declaration.
questionData.explanation: Lists use square brackets in Python.

challenges.funFact: Git was created by Linus Torvalds in 2005.

challenges.title: Input Basics
challenges.description: Find the right function for user text.

questionData.questionText: Which function reads input from the user?
questionData.explanation: input() reads a line from standard input.

challenges.funFact: Many terminal commands are older than graphical interfaces.

challenges.title: Range Stop

questionData.questionText: What does range(3) generate?
questionData.explanation: range(n) starts at 0 and excludes n.

challenges.funFact: Compilers can optimize code better than many humans by hand.

challenges.title: Append Call

questionData.questionText: Fill method to add one item to list a.
questionData.explanation: append adds one element to the list end.

challenges.funFact: The first domain name ever registered was symbolics.com.

challenges.title: Indentation Bug

questionData.questionText: Select the properly indented print line.
questionData.explanation: Python blocks must be indented consistently.

challenges.funFact: Whitespace is syntax in Python, unlike many other languages.

challenges.title: Type Cast

questionData.questionText: How do you convert '42' to int?
questionData.explanation: int() converts numeric strings to integers.

challenges.funFact: Unicode lets computers represent text from almost every writing system.

challenges.title: String Upper

questionData.questionText: Complete method to uppercase a string s.
questionData.explanation: upper() returns uppercase version of a string.

challenges.funFact: ASCII originally used 7 bits, not 8.

challenges.title: Dictionary Access

questionData.questionText: How do you access key 'age' in dict user?
questionData.explanation: Use square bracket indexing for dict keys.

challenges.funFact: Hash maps provide average O(1) lookup time.

challenges.title: Else Colon Bug

questionData.questionText: Which line fixes the else statement?
questionData.explanation: Control flow blocks require a colon at the end.

challenges.funFact: A single missing character causes many syntax errors.

challenges.title: Join Method

questionData.questionText: Fill the separator in a join call.
questionData.explanation: join() combines iterable strings with a separator.

challenges.funFact: Functional programming concepts are popular in modern languages.

challenges.title: While Condition

questionData.questionText: Which while loop runs until i reaches 5?
questionData.explanation: Use i < 5 for 0 to 4 style loops.

challenges.funFact: Infinite loops are useful in event-driven systems when controlled.

challenges.title: Return Bug

questionData.questionText: Pick the corrected return statement.
questionData.fixedCode:
def add(a, b):
    return a + b
questionData.bugDescription: Function prints instead of returning value.
questionData.explanation: Use return when a function should provide a value.

challenges.funFact: Pure functions are easier to test and reason about.

challenges.title: Enumerate Use

questionData.questionText: Which gives both index and value in a loop?
questionData.explanation: enumerate pairs each item with its index.

challenges.funFact: Good naming beats clever code in most code reviews.

challenges.title: Exception Keyword

questionData.questionText: Fill the keyword to handle exceptions.
questionData.explanation: except catches matching runtime exceptions.

challenges.funFact: Structured error handling improves resilience.

challenges.title: Tuple Bug

questionData.questionText: Fix single-item tuple declaration.
questionData.bugDescription: Single-item tuple needs a trailing comma.
questionData.explanation: Without comma, (5) is just an int expression.

challenges.funFact: Tiny punctuation marks can alter data types completely.

challenges.title: List Comprehension

questionData.questionText: Which creates squares from nums?
questionData.explanation: That is correct Python list comprehension syntax.

challenges.funFact: Comprehensions can be both concise and performant.

challenges.title: Keyword-Only Hint

questionData.questionText: Fill the marker enabling keyword-only params after it.
questionData.codeSnippet:
def f(a, ____, b):
    return a + b
questionData.explanation: Arguments after * are keyword-only parameters.

challenges.funFact: API design often depends on parameter clarity and intent.

challenges.title: Mutable Default Bug

questionData.questionText: Choose the safer default-arg pattern.
questionData.fixedCode:
def add_item(item, bucket=None):
    bucket = [] if bucket is None else bucket
    bucket.append(item)
    return bucket
questionData.bugDescription: Mutable default list persists across calls.
questionData.explanation: Use None then create a fresh list inside function.

challenges.funFact: Many subtle bugs come from shared mutable state.

challenges.title: Generator Expression

questionData.questionText: Which expression is a generator?
questionData.explanation: Parenthesized comprehension creates a generator.

challenges.funFact: Generators help process large datasets with less memory.

challenges.title: Context Manager Blank

questionData.questionText: Fill keyword for context-managed file open.
questionData.explanation: with ensures the file is closed automatically.

challenges.funFact: Context managers reduce resource leaks.

challenges.title: Shadowing Bug

questionData.questionText: Pick the best variable rename.
questionData.explanation: Avoid naming variables after built-in functions.

challenges.funFact: Shadowing builtins can cause confusing runtime errors.

challenges.title: Async Keyword

questionData.questionText: Which line declares an async function in Python?
questionData.explanation: Use async def for coroutine function definitions.

challenges.funFact: Async IO helps servers scale with many concurrent tasks.
